title: Align existing documentation with implementation and evidence
outOfScope: Source code, tests, CI, release metadata, and runtime behavior.
Claims of universal semantic detection, sandbox isolation, identity/authentication, immutable audit, or enterprise compliance proof.

sources.reason: Deterministic evidence and non-claim boundary source.
Runtime installation and calibration entrypoint source.

riskAssessment.reason: Documentation-only alignment against existing deterministic implementation and bounded trust claims.

executionDecision.reason: The user authorized serial execution of this documentation Work Item; Work Item 1 remains out of scope.

preReviewWarnings: Check every claim against implementation/evidence and preserve explicit enterprise-boundary NO-GO language.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: README.md states the bounded governance purpose and lifecycle accurately.
README.ja.md and README.zh-CN.md use the same bounded terminology.
docs/architecture.md states the repository architecture boundary accurately.
docs/configuration.md states the repository configuration boundary accurately.
docs/installation.md states that runtime installation is not calibration completion.
docs/upgrade.md states Impact Assessment and Active Configuration preservation.
docs/trust-layer.md preserves deterministic known-risk and semantic-risk boundaries.
make check-docs-metadata passes for all changed Markdown files.
make quality passes after the documentation changes.
guidelines: Documentation claims must be backed by repository implementation or evidence.
Do not expand product/security/compliance claims beyond the documented boundary.

intent.problem: Existing documentation must reflect the completed lifecycle and trust-layer changes without overstating deterministic governance as universal semantic or enterprise security capability.
constraints: Documentation-only implementation; do not change runtime behavior or Work Item 1.
Runtime installation is not calibration completion; updates require Impact Assessment before recalibration.
Default language is Japanese and old Active Configuration remains valid until Candidate activation succeeds.
intent.rationale: A single evidence-backed documentation pass reduces drift between the implementation, release/lifecycle evidence, multilingual docs, and the explicit enterprise boundary.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Known deterministic lifecycle claims match implementation and evidence.
Runtime install, calibration, update impact assessment, and active configuration semantics are distinguished.
Enterprise/security boundary and semantic-risk non-claims remain explicit across requested languages.

rawUserRequest: Execute the current recalibration plan serially through complete Work Item lifecycles, with Work Item 1 not started; align existing documentation as the next Work Item.

declaredIntent.summary: Align existing documentation with implementation and evidence while preserving bounded trust and enterprise boundary claims.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: Set only when a human explicitly approves restricted governance paths.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
